# SC-17: Deployment Integrity

**Track:** Security Controls (`SC`) · **Priority:** MUST (mandatory) · **Status:** draft

Cryptographically verify that the artefacts being deployed to production are byte-for-byte identical to the artefacts that were tested in Stage 4. No tampering, substitution, or modification between test and production deployment is permitted. Verification must occur immediately before deployment execution.

## Required In Stages

| Stage | Name | Applicability |
|-------|------|---------------|
| 5 | Deployment & Release | Mandatory |

## Delegation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Pattern** | Fully automated |
| **Agent does** | Compute and compare cryptographic checksums of test and deployment artefacts; block deployment if any mismatch. |
| **Human does** | Investigate any detected integrity failures before deployment can proceed. |

## Outputs

| Artifact | Type | Description | Retention |
|----------|------|-------------|-----------|
| Deployment integrity verification record | evidence | Cryptographic hash comparison confirming test and production artefacts are identical. | 7 years (DORA) |


## Automation

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Frequency** | per_release |
| **SLA** | — |
| **Tooling** | — |

## Failure Behaviour

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **On failure** | block |
| **Escalate to** | Security Architect |

Any integrity mismatch immediately blocks deployment. Security incident process is initiated.

## Dependencies

- `SC-16`


## Regulatory Mapping

| Regulation | Article | Obligation |
|------------|---------|------------|
| DORA | Art. 9(2) | ICT system integrity must be maintained through the deployment process |
| DORA | Art. 28a | Supply chain security requirements for ICT systems |
| EU AI Act | Art. 15(3) | Resilience against manipulation of AI systems by third parties during deployment |


## Ownership

**Owner team:** Security / Release Management · **Owner role:** Security Architect
